title: Freedom of navigation in the Red Sea
text: The Red Sea and the Bab-el-Mandeb strait form one of the world's most important shipping lanes, linking the Suez Canal to the Indian Ocean. Roughly twelve percent of global trade transits the corridor. Attacks on commercial vessels threaten freedom of navigation and push shipping companies to reroute around the Cape of Good Hope, raising costs and delivery times. The United States stresses deterrence and the protection of maritime trade routes.
